name: Joel Dy
role: Free Tasting Interest · No event scoped

handling.tone: Long-game tasting tire-kicker. Don't pressure for an event he doesn't have. Match Andre's last 'what are you trying to get clear on' — open without forcing.
handling.voice_adjustments: Andre voice: light, no urgency, low-pressure. Don't spam. One thoughtful nudge then dormant queue.
handling.context: Opted into Free Tasting form on website Apr 18. No event date, no venue, no guest count. Tasting-curious, not buying-ready. Best outcome: he attends a tasting, has great experience, remembers Comeketo when his real event materializes.

context_anchors: Free Tasting Opt-In only — no event scoped
No phone, email, location captured in breakdown
Long-game lead — months out, not weeks

lead.next_move: One warm low-pressure message giving binary: specific event or just curious

enrichment.identity_notes: Dy is uncommon Asian (Filipino/Chinese-Filipino) surname. Short last name + simple first = limited public footprint. Could be future customer, foodie, vague event-thinker, or industry recon.
win_angles: Don't force an event he doesn't have
Tasting itself is the product right now — May 3 / May 17 are low-stakes demos
Patience: warm-list expansion play, multi-year asset
If he tastes, he's a known prospect who liked Comeketo
nepq_openers: Continuation: 'Hey Joel — quick one. Are you scoping us out for an event you're starting to plan, or in see-if-Comeketo-is-interesting mode? Both fine — just want to give you the right next step.'
Frame: 'Tasting opt-ins fall into three buckets — actively planning, vaguely planning, food-curious. Where do you land?'
Tasting anchor: '$35 a head, $70 of which becomes credit if you ever book.'
open_gaps: Whether there's an event he hasn't told us about
enrichment.strategic_note: Long-game. Best outcome: tasting with great experience, books real event later. One thoughtful message giving the binary, then either advance to tasting or deprioritize. The 50% confidence opp is fictional — should be 5-10% until there's an actual event. Don't burn cycles on no-event tire-kickers.

notes: No event = no clock — patience play
Tasting attendance = warm-list expansion regardless of booking
Drop to long-term/dormant queue if no engagement after one more nudge
